nativeRange.habitat: Shallow to subtidal reef zones on rocky and rubble substrate; photic zone

imageCandidates.notes: Photo by Héctor Ibáñez Cantero, 2048x1536. Clean, sharp whole clump isolated against dark negative space; clearly shows the calcified reddish-pink dichotomous tubular branches with pale growing tips. Recorded under accepted synonym Tricleocarpa fragilis.
Photo by Katerina Kalogerini, 2048x1536. Best colour match — vivid rose/reddish-pink tubular dichotomous branches filling the frame; some turf algae around edges. Synonym Tricleocarpa fragilis.

Photo by Sarah Faulwetter, 2048x1536. In-situ reef habitat shot of a whole spherical/bushy reddish-pink thallus on rock, giving growth-form context; slightly hazy water. Synonym Tricleocarpa fragilis.

summary: Pink Succulent Thicket (Galaxaura oblongata) is a rare calcareous red macroalgae with thick, upright reddish-pink branches in an open, spherical growth form — more open and succulent-looking than the related Galaxaura rugosa. Rarely encountered in the hobby, it demands stable calcium, alkalinity, and high light.
careNotes: Pink Succulent Thicket is a calcareous red macroalgae in the genus Galaxaura (family Galaxauraceae, order Nemaliales, class Florideophyceae, division Rhodophyta). Its thick, upright branching form is notably more open than the more compact growth of the related Galaxaura rugosa (Tubular Thicket). The thallus is reddish-pink and grows in a broadly spherical arrangement of succulent-looking branches, reaching approximately 2.5–12.5 cm. As a fully calcified red alga, G. oblongata deposits calcium carbonate into its thallus; stable calcium (generally advised around 400–450 ppm) and magnesium (around 1250–1350 ppm) are considered important for long-term health. These targets are extrapolated from family-level Galaxauraceae husbandry, as no species-specific aquarium data are currently available; individual results may vary. Standard reef parameters are advised: SG 1.023–1.026, temperature 24–27°C, pH 8.1–8.4, kH 8–12 dKH. High lighting tends to be beneficial for this growth form, and placement in a well-lit area of the display tank is commonly recommended for calcified red macroalgae. Growth rate is generally slow, consistent with the metabolic cost of skeletal calcification. Propagation in the aquarium is typically by fragmentation of the branching thallus. Care data are informed by family-level knowledge of Galaxauraceae and comparison with related specimens including Galaxaura sp. ([sw-algae-020]) and Dichotomaria marginata ([sw-algae-041]). Caribbean distribution is inferred from the family's known range and is consistent with other aquarium Galaxauraceae; the exact regional provenance of aquarium specimens may vary. As a red alga, this species will MELT at the base if buried in sand — attach to rock via superglue, zip-tie/fishing line, or wedging instead. (Sand-bed planting is generally limited to genera such as Caulerpa, Halimeda, Penicillus, Rhipocephalus, and Udotea, plus some calcified Dasycladales.)

primary.name: Mosaic Macros
primary.notes: Sole hobby source. Confirmed scientific name Galaxaura oblongata, trade name, and morphology (thick upright branching, reddish-pink, spherical growth, 1–5"+ cutting sizes). Noted shipping sensitivity. Minimal husbandry parameters on product page.

additional.name: Sibling cross-reference — Bramble Thicket (sw-algae-020)
additional.notes: Congener Galaxaura sp. Used for family-level husbandry extrapolation (waterParameters, careLevel, placement, propagation). Both are Galaxauraceae, calcified, display-placement macroalgae.

additional.name: Sibling cross-reference — Flat-Branched Pink Thicket (sw-algae-041)
additional.notes: Sister genus Dichotomaria marginata (Galaxauraceae). Used for family-level husbandry extrapolation, lighting (high), and Caribbean distribution confirmation.

additional.name: Mosaic Macros general macroalgae care guide
additional.notes: Slice-wide secondary source — provides authoritative Mosaic-wide parameter targets (temp 22-26°C, pH 8.1-8.4, Ca 400-450 ppm, Mg 1250-1350 ppm, NO3 5-20 ppm, PO4 0.02-0.1 ppm) and the 4-method propagation standard. Added retroactively 2026-06-08.
